description: GitHub is a web-based hosting service for version control using Git, providing repository hosting, issue tracking, pull requests, and collaboration features for software development.
authentication: Supports username/password, personal access tokens (PATs), OAuth apps, GitHub Apps, and SAML SSO for enterprise accounts.
self_serve: Yes – users can sign up, create repositories, and configure settings without requiring assistance.
api_surface: Extensive REST API and GraphQL API covering repositories, issues, pull requests, webhooks, actions, and more.
mcp: GitHub Marketplace offers third‑party integrations and apps that extend functionality.
buildability: GitHub Actions provides CI/CD pipelines, runners, and workflow automation directly within the platform.
main_blocker: Complex permission and access control models can be challenging to configure for large teams.
